locations.name: 班克斯半島
locations.summary: 班克斯半島（Banks Peninsula）位於紐西蘭南島東海岸坎特伯雷地區，部份為太平洋所環繞，並和南島的最大城市基督城臨接。半島的陸地面積約1,000平方公里。此半島和附近的利特爾頓自1989年開始由班克斯半島地方政府管轄直到在200…

locations.name: 公园
locations.summary: 公園是供公眾消遣遊憩的場所。公園可以指下列場所： 市街地的一區，栽植草木或設置噴水池的遊憩場所。 市街地的一角，設置兒童遊樂器材或體育設施的兒童公園及體育公園，即遊樂場。

relations.subject: New Zealand bellbird
relations.evidence: |last3=Ji |first3=Weihong |date=2008 |title=Assessing natural dispersal of New Zealand bellbirds using song type and song playbacks |url=https://newzealandecology.org/nzje/2872 |journal=New Zealand Journal of Ecology |volume=32 |issue=2 |pages=147–154 |ref=Brunton2008}} left|thumb|Male bellbird on a kōwhai tree, [[Banks Peninsula]] The bellbird's habitat encompasses both native and exotic forests and scrublands, and it is frequently found in urban areas such as parks and gardens, especially if there is a nearby patch of native bush

relations.subject: New Zealand bellbird
relations.evidence: At the time of European arrival, they were one of the most common birds. Bellbird populations have declined significantly due to the destruction of native forests (initiated by Māori and perpetuated by Europeans) and the introduction of invasive predators such as cats (Felis catus), mustelids including ferrets (Mustela furo), stoats (M
relations.object_summary: 岛屿鸟类常受猫、鼬和鼠等外来种影响。

relations.subject: New Zealand bellbird
relations.evidence: nivalis), and rodents such as the brown rat (Rattus norvegicus) and the black rat (Rattus rattus). Between the 1860s and about 1900, bellbird numbers declined very dramatically, prompting many ornithologists to search for reasons beyond deforestation and introduced mammals. Walter Buller, the prominent New Zealand ornithologist of the 19th century, in 1871 quoted Hokianga resident Frederick Maning saying: The decline started in the north of the country and moved south. By 1870, bellbirds had vanished from Northland, and between 1868 and 1878 they disappeared from Great Barrier Island. In the South Island, bellbird numbers in Canterbury did not reach their lowest point until 1900
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

relations.subject: New Zealand bellbird
relations.evidence: |date=2021 |title=Conservation status of birds in Aotearoa New Zealand, 2021 |url=https://www.doc.govt.nz/globalassets/documents/science-and-technical/nztcs36entire.pdf |website=Department of Conservation Te Papa Atawhai}} According to IUCN and a 2010 study, the overall bellbird population is suspected to be in decline as a result of ongoing habitat destruction and predation by introduced mammals. The decline is most prominent in lowland areas under 1000 metres in elevation. A 2022 study of New Zealand garden birds concluded that bellbird numbers are stable or locally increasing.{{Cite journal |last1=MacLeod |first1=Catriona J
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
